action.reasoning: Observation: iter29 train produced all-zero loss/val AP=0 due to truncated train.py (incomplete at 'def com', no full loop/loss/scheduler), overwriting best.pt with broken model; eval failed on state_dict mismatch (layers.20 [2048,2048] in ckpt vs [128,2048] in 6-layer model from prior 7-layer overwrites). Best remains 0.2191 (val~0.233) from iter27 with FP(r2+r3)+desc+2048/6-layer/0.1. No jsonl in recent runs (outdated curves), PyG still segfaults. Analysis: Truncation + arch drift root cause of collapse; masked BCE, DataLoader, per-epoch logging, proper nan handling (torch.where), ReduceLROnPlateau on val_ap, grad_clip are proven stable from earlier successes. Decision: Fully rewrite complete non-truncated train.py (match exact best arch=4106/2048/6/0.1, robust masked loss, sklearn AP with filters, jsonl with {'step':epoch,...}, AdamW+patience=25, 150 epochs, best.pt save); keep model.py/predict.py (consistent); run train (uses caches, ~20min), then submit_eval on new best.pt to recover+improve avg_precision before budget ends.
